page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor configuration includes 32 KB of L1 instruction cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, scoring 2923.48, which places it in the top 25%. However, its multi-core CPU performance of 2928.18 is categorized as weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth at 136.13 GB/sec and large block uncached read/write speeds falling into the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance rank in the bottom 10%.

Given these performance characteristics, the c2d-standard-2 is suited for workloads that rely on single-threaded execution rather than highly parallelized or memory-intensive operations. It achieves average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. However, it is poorly suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and heavy database operations due to its low multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth. This instance represents an entry-level compute option where single-core execution speed is prioritized over multi-threaded throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It allocates 1 physical core with 2 threads and 32 MB of L3 cache, without bundled local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU rating, but weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing perform at average levels, whereas database operations, software compilation, and Redis throughput are significantly constrained. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and entry-level compute tasks that do not require high memory throughput or parallel processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It features strong single-core CPU performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 features 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong single-core but weak multi-core performance.
tagline: An entry-level compute instance offering strong single-core CPU performance balanced with basic memory capacity for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Weak multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and light text processing
